title: SEGMENT 16 — Gene therapy on HSCs: Casgevy, Zynteglo, Skysona, Lyfgenia

lines.text: SEGMENT 16 — Gene therapy on HSCs: Casgevy, Zynteglo, Skysona, Lyfgenia
In December 2023, the FDA approved two gene therapies for sickle cell disease on the same day.
Casgevy, from Vertex and CRISPR Therapeutics, is the first CRISPR-based therapy ever approved.
Lyfgenia, from bluebird bio, is a lentiviral gene therapy.
Both work by editing the patient's own hematopoietic stem cells.
Both run on transplant infrastructure.
Let me describe Casgevy, because it's the cleanest example.
The patient is mobilized with G-CSF and plerixafor.
CD34+ cells are collected by apheresis.

The cells are shipped to a manufacturing facility where they're electroporated with a CRISPR-Cas9 ribonucleoprotein complex targeting the BCL11A enhancer — a regulatory element that normally suppresses fetal hemoglobin in adults.
Editing this enhancer reactivates fetal hemoglobin production, which doesn't sickle and substitutes for the defective adult hemoglobin.
The edited cells are expanded, quality-controlled, and shipped back.
The patient receives myeloablative busulfan conditioning, and the edited cells are infused.
They engraft, rebuild the marrow, and produce red cells with high levels of fetal hemoglobin.
The disease is functionally cured.
The clinical results have been dramatic.
In the pivotal trials, the great majority of patients had no vaso-occlusive crises in the year following treatment.
Some patients have now been followed for five-plus years with sustained benefit.
It is, in essence, autologous HSCT plus a CRISPR step.
Zynteglo (betibeglogene autotemcel), from bluebird, treats transfusion-dependent beta-thalassemia by using a lentiviral vector to add a functional beta-globin gene to the patient's HSCs.
Same infrastructure — mobilize, collect, transduce, condition, reinfuse.
Approved 2022.
Skysona (elivaldogene autotemcel), also from bluebird, treats cerebral adrenoleukodystrophy in boys by lentiviral addition of the ABCD1 gene to autologous HSCs.
The transduced myeloid cells cross into the brain and deliver functional protein.
Approved 2022.
Lyfgenia is bluebird's lentiviral gene therapy for sickle cell, approved 2023, adding a modified beta-globin (T87Q) that doesn't sickle.
Strimvelis, from GSK and originally San Raffaele in Milan, is a gamma-retroviral gene therapy for ADA-SCID, approved in Europe in 2016.
Same paradigm: autologous HSCs, gene-corrected, reinfused.
Each of these is HSCT with a gene-editing or gene-addition step.
The conditioning, the harvest, the engraftment monitoring, the inpatient stay — all of that is transplant.
The gene therapy is the payload.
The transplant is the chassis.
